title: JavaFX Refactorer Handoff
description: Independent validation schema for the refactor-handoff.json file produced by javafx-refactorer. This schema validates the handoff artifact consumed by javafx-developer in Fix Consumption mode, separate from the refactoring report schema. Field names are aligned with report-templates/report-schema.json, and each refactoring_plan item includes the unified Fix Handoff fields (target_file, target_lines, fix_type, fix_priority, code_fingerprint, anchor_pattern, ast_node_signature) so it can be consumed by the developer's Fix Consumption Protocol without conversion.

project.description: Name of the project being refactored

refactorer_version.description: Refactorer skill version. Fixed at 1.0 for this handoff format.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the handoff file was created (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-06-30T10:00:00Z)

scope.description: Refactoring scope: full project, a specific package, or specific files. Values match the scope definition in javafx-refactorer/SKILL.md.

smell_catalog.description: Detected code smells with location, severity, and category. Field names are aligned with report-templates/report-schema.json for consistency. Each entry is referenced by refactoring_plan items via smell_ids.

smell_id.description: Unique smell identifier, e.g., SMELL-001. Field name aligned with report-schema.

type.description: Code smell type. Enum values are sourced from report-templates/report-schema.json to ensure consistency.

file.description: Source file path where the smell was detected

lines.description: Line range of the smell location, e.g., '1-850' or '42'. Field name aligned with report-schema.

ast_node_signature.description: AST-level anchor: fully qualified method/field/class signature (e.g., com.example.controller.UserController). Null for non-Java files. Uses the same format as the unified Fix Handoff ast_node_signature defined in javafx-orchestrator/SKILL.md.

severity.description: Severity of the code smell, consistent with the classification in javafx-refactorer/SKILL.md

detail.description: Detailed description of the detected smell, including metrics (e.g., line count, method count, LCOM score). Field name aligned with report-schema.

recommended_refactoring.description: Refactoring action ID recommended for this smell

refactoring_plan.description: Prioritized refactoring actions. Each action includes the unified Fix Handoff fields (target_file, target_lines, fix_type, fix_priority, code_fingerprint, anchor_pattern, ast_node_signature) so it can be consumed by javafx-developer's Fix Consumption Protocol without conversion. Field names for smell reference, action type, and snippets are aligned with report-templates/report-schema.json.

refactor_id.description: Unique refactoring action identifier, e.g., RF-001. Field name aligned with report-schema.

type.description: Refactoring action type. Field name and enum values aligned with report-schema refactoring_plan.type.

smell_ids.description: Smell IDs from smell_catalog that this refactoring action addresses

target_file.description: Primary source file targeted by this refactoring action. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

target_lines.description: Line range in the target file to modify, e.g., '45-60' or '78'. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

fix_type.description: Fix operation type. Refactoring actions typically use 'replace' (replace problematic code with refactored version) or 'insert' (add new extracted class/file). Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

fix_priority.description: Fix priority for batch ordering (1 = highest). Derived from the refactoring action priority and phase: phase_1_immediate actions get priority 1-2, phase_2_high get 3-5, etc. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

code_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 hash of the problematic code snippet (whitespace-normalized). Used for drift-resistant matching in the developer's 4-level location hierarchy. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

anchor_pattern.description: Surrounding context signature (2 lines before + 2 lines after the target code) for secondary location matching. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

ast_node_signature.description: AST-level anchor: fully qualified method or class signature (e.g., com.example.controller.UserController#saveUser(ActionEvent)). Enables refactor-resistant location matching. Null for non-Java files. Uses the same format as the unified Fix Handoff ast_node_signature defined in javafx-orchestrator/SKILL.md. Unified Fix Handoff field — consumed by developer Fix Consumption Protocol.

description.description: Human-readable description of the refactoring action

new_files.description: New files created by this refactoring action (e.g., extracted class file paths)

risk.description: Risk level of behavior change for this refactoring action

priority.description: Refactoring priority (1 = highest). Used to derive fix_priority for the unified Fix Handoff format.

dependencies.description: Refactoring action IDs that must be applied before this action (safe sequencing)

before_snippet.description: Code snippet showing the problematic code before refactoring. Field name aligned with report-schema.

after_snippet.description: Code snippet showing the refactored code after applying this action. Field name aligned with report-schema.

behavior_equivalence_check.description: Per-action behavior equivalence verification. Field name and structure aligned with report-schema.

method_signatures_preserved.description: Whether public API method signatures are preserved by this refactoring action

call_sites_to_update.description: Call sites that must be updated due to moved or renamed methods

new_public_api.description: New public API entries introduced by this refactoring action

side_effect_analysis.description: Side-effect classification of the refactored code. 'none' = pure function, no side effects; 'logging' = logging only; 'state_mutation' = modifies shared state; 'io' = performs I/O (file, network, DB); 'unknown' = cannot determine statically. Used to detect behavior divergence when inlining or moving code with side effects to new call sites.

tech_debt.description: Technical debt inventory, metrics, and phased repayment plan

total_items.description: Total number of detected debt items (smells)

critical.description: Count of Critical severity debt items

major.description: Count of Major severity debt items

minor.description: Count of Minor severity debt items

estimated_effort_hours.description: Estimated total repayment effort in hours

debt_density_per_kloc.description: Debt items per 1000 lines of code

repayment_plan.description: Phased repayment plan referencing refactoring action IDs

phase_1_immediate.description: Priority 1 items: Critical smells to fix immediately (e.g., RF-001)

phase_2_high.description: Priority 2 items: Major smells to fix in the next sprint

phase_3_medium.description: Priority 3 items: Minor smells to fix when touching relevant code

phase_4_low.description: Priority 4 items: Cosmetic improvements during code reviews

verification_plan.description: Pre-refactor baseline and post-refactor test expectations, plus semantic checks

pre_refactor_tests.description: Command to run the pre-refactor test baseline, e.g., 'mvn test'

baseline_results.description: Test results captured before refactoring is applied

post_refactor_expectation.description: Human-readable expectation for post-refactor test results

semantic_checks.description: Semantic equivalence checks to perform, e.g., signature_preservation, call_site_integrity, field_access_integrity, import_graph_acyclic

behavior_equivalence_check.description: Overall behavior equivalence verification approach spanning all refactoring actions. Per-action checks are in each refactoring_plan[].behavior_equivalence_check.

method_signatures_preserved.description: Whether public API method signatures are preserved across all refactoring actions

call_sites_to_update.description: Aggregated call sites that must be updated due to moved or renamed methods

new_public_api.description: Aggregated new public API entries introduced by the refactoring

side_effect_analysis_summary.description: Aggregated side-effect analysis across all refactoring actions. Maps each side_effect_category to the count of actions with that classification.

none.description: Number of actions classified as pure (no side effects)

logging.description: Number of actions with logging side effects

state_mutation.description: Number of actions with state mutation side effects

io.description: Number of actions with I/O side effects

unknown.description: Number of actions with undetermined side effects

developer_instructions.description: Instructions for javafx-developer on how to apply the refactoring safely. The developer consumes this handoff via Fix Consumption Protocol (Step 5.5), which recognizes the unified Fix Handoff fields in each refactoring_plan item.

apply_order.description: Recommended application order for the refactoring actions, respecting dependencies

safety_constraints.description: Safety constraints the developer must follow, e.g., compile after each action, run tests after each phase
